nodes.context: Hmong street gang created in 1988, active in California and Midwest, involved in drug trafficking and violence.
One of the first Hmong gangs in California, formed in 1985 in Fresno, from which MOD splintered.
Hmong gang that splintered from Peace MOD along with MOD and Oriental Ruthless Boys.
Hmong gang that splintered from Peace MOD; rival of MOD.
One of Minnesota's first Hmong gangs, eventually overtaken by MOD.
Rival gang to MOD in Sacramento, California.
Junior version of MOD; listed as ally in infobox.
Mexican drug cartel that smuggled methamphetamine to Minnesota and worked with MOD.
Gang involved in dispute with MOD in Wisconsin.
Gang involved in dispute with MOD in Wisconsin; listed as rival in infobox.
Gang that had turf war with MOD in Fresno, California; listed as rival in infobox.
Gang that shot at MOD member's home in Eau Claire, Wisconsin; listed as rival in infobox.
Hmong gang involved in turf war with MOD in Milwaukee; listed as rival in infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1988, a handful of Hmong youth broke off from one of the first Hmong gangs in California known as "Peace MOD", originally formed in 1985 in Fresno, California to combat other gangs preying upon the Hmong community. The splinter group formed the "Masters of Destruction" (MOD).
the breakup of "Peace MOD" created rivalries between all three of them.
edges.context: MOD and MBS are rival gangs that formed from the breakup of Peace MOD.

edges.evidence_quote: the breakup of "Peace MOD" created rivalries between all three of them.
edges.context: MOD and ORB are rival gangs that formed from the breakup of Peace MOD.

edges.evidence_quote: one of Minnesota's first Hmong gang known as the White Tigers was eventually overtaken by MOD's presence in the Twin Cities.

tensions between the Menace of Destruction and the Asian Family Gangsters were intense. Following the death of a young M.O.D gang member and a shooting at several Tiny Little Rascals; a junior version of M.O.D, the M.O.D retaliated by performing a drive-by shooting in North Sacramento Asian Family Gangsters' turf, killing a rival gang member.
edges.context: MOD and Asian Family Gangsters were in conflict, with MOD retaliating with a drive-by shooting.
Tiny Little Rascals is listed as an ally of MOD in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: The cartel La Familia smuggled millions of dollars' worth of methamphetamine from Mexico to Minnesota. The drugs then went through a local member of the cartel and the Menace of Destruction gang.
edges.context: La Familia and MOD cooperated in drug trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: the M.O.D was involved in a dispute with two other gangs; the Laotian Posse and the Alliance.
edges.context: MOD was in dispute with Laotian Posse.

edges.evidence_quote: the M.O.D was involved in a dispute with two other gangs; the Laotian Posse and the Alliance.
edges.context: MOD was in dispute with Alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: M.O.D members were migrating to the Midwestern states while having a turf war with Tiny Rascal Gang in Fresno, California.
edges.context: MOD had a turf war with Tiny Rascal Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Several members of the Ruthless Crip Gangsters drove to the Eau Claire home of a member of the gang Menace of Destruction. Three to five shots were fired at a man after a verbal confrontation, one shot narrowly missing his head.
edges.context: Ruthless Crip Gangsters shot at a MOD member's home.

edges.evidence_quote: M.O.D. and other Hmong gangs such as Asian Crips were involved in a turf war with each other, which resulted in more than 120 shootings in the area.
edges.context: MOD and Asian Crips were in a turf war.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Alliance, Asian Crips, Asian Family Gangsters, Fresno Bulldogs, Hmong Nation Society, Junior Criminal Crips, Laotian Posse, Oriental Ruthless Boys, Oroville Mono Boys, Ruthless Crip Gangsters, Tiny Rascal Gang
edges.context: Fresno Bulldogs is listed as a rival in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Alliance, Asian Crips, Asian Family Gangsters, Fresno Bulldogs, Hmong Nation Society, Junior Criminal Crips, Laotian Posse, Oriental Ruthless Boys, Oroville Mono Boys, Ruthless Crip Gangsters, Tiny Rascal Gang
edges.context: Hmong Nation Society is listed as a rival in the infobox.

edges.context: Junior Criminal Crips is listed as a rival in the infobox.
